message:
fix(llm): repair broken double-checked locking initialization

The LLM initialization code used _router variable for the guard check, but
_router was explicitly set to None at the end, causing initialization to
run on every call. This could lead to:
1. Redundant initialization work
2. Duplicate callbacks being added to litellm.success_callback

Changes:
- Add _initialized boolean flag for proper double-checked locking
- Guard checks now correctly prevent re-initialization
- Remove redundant `from contextlib import suppress` import
- Add type narrowing assertions in test_contact_management_flow.py

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@
 
 _router: Optional[Any] = None
 _init_lock = asyncio.Lock()
+_initialized: bool = False
 _logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)
 
 
@@ -82,11 +83,11 @@ def _on_success(kwargs: dict[str, Any], completion_response: Any, start_time: fl
 
 
 async def _ensure_initialized() -> None:
-    global _router
-    if _router is not None:
+    global _router, _initialized
+    if _initialized:
         return
     async with _init_lock:
-        if _router is not None:
+        if _initialized:
             return
         settings = get_settings()
 
@@ -98,9 +99,7 @@ async def _ensure_initialized() -> None:
 
         # Enable cache globally (in-memory or Redis) using LiteLLM's API
         if settings.llm.cache_enabled:
-            from contextlib import suppress
-
-            with suppress(Exception):
+            with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
                 backend = (getattr(settings.llm, "cache_backend", "local") or "local").lower()
                 if backend == "redis" and getattr(settings.llm, "cache_redis_url", ""):
                     parsed = urlparse(settings.llm.cache_redis_url)
@@ -128,6 +127,7 @@ async def _ensure_initialized() -> None:
         # Router is designed for load balancing across multiple deployments with a model_list,
         # but we're just using single API keys, so direct completion is simpler and works fine.
         _router = None
+        _initialized = True
 
 
 def _choose_best_available_model(preferred: str) -> str:

@@ -201,6 +201,8 @@ async def test_request_contact_creates_pending_link(isolated_env):
         # Verify database record
         agent_a_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_a)
         agent_b_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_b)
+        assert agent_a_id is not None, "Agent A should exist"
+        assert agent_b_id is not None, "Agent B should exist"
         link = await get_agent_link_from_db(agent_a_id, agent_b_id)
         assert link is not None
         assert link["status"] == "pending"

+        assert agent_b_id is not None, "Agent B should exist"
         link = await get_agent_link_from_db(agent_a_id, agent_b_id)
         assert link is not None
         assert link["status"] == "approved"
@@ -375,6 +379,8 @@ async def test_deny_contact_request(isolated_env):
         # Verify database shows blocked (status is "blocked" when denied)
         agent_a_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_a)
         agent_b_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_b)
+        assert agent_a_id is not None, "Agent A should exist"
+        assert agent_b_id is not None, "Agent B should exist"
         link = await get_agent_link_from_db(agent_a_id, agent_b_id)
         assert link is not None
         assert link["status"] in ("denied", "blocked"), f"Expected denied/blocked, got {link['status']}"
@@ -474,6 +480,7 @@ async def test_policy_open_allows_all_messages(isolated_env):
 
         # Verify policy was set
         project_id = await get_project_id(project_key)
+        assert project_id is not None, "Project should exist"
         policy = await get_agent_policy(project_id, agent_b)
         assert policy == "open"

         policy = await get_agent_policy(project_id, agent_b)

@@ -687,6 +696,8 @@ async def test_contact_request_has_ttl(isolated_env):
         # Verify expires_ts is set in database
         agent_a_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_a)
         agent_b_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_b)

@@ -798,6 +809,8 @@ async def test_macro_contact_handshake_auto_accept(isolated_env):
         # Check database for approved status
         agent_a_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_a)
         agent_b_id = await get_agent_id(project_key, agent_b)
+        assert agent_a_id is not None, "Agent A should exist"

         link = await get_agent_link_from_db(agent_a_id, agent_b_id)
         if link:
             assert link["status"] == "approved"

         project_id = await get_project_id(project_key)
